$comment: Six adventures = six ways people use agents. Each has a Party (ideal team, full sheets), NPCs (allies/adjacent, full sheets), and a Bestiary (failure-mode monsters, stubs). Monsters recur across adventures, same as a module's bestiary. Persona ids must match data/characters.json.

adventures.name: The Forge of Endless Diffs
adventures.quest: A repo with no tests, a deadline, and a thousand ways to over-build. Ship the feature without burning the codebase down.

adventures.name: The Self-Winding Labyrinth
adventures.quest: Give it a goal and walk away. The labyrinth rewards the team that loops with a leash — and devours the one that doesn't.

adventures.name: The Boundless Library
adventures.quest: Every answer is in here somewhere, next to a thousand convincing forgeries. Return with the truth, and a citation for it.

adventures.name: Whispers in the Hall of Echoes
adventures.quest: The Hall answers in your own voice. Some echoes comfort, some manipulate, and one in extended session forgets it is only an echo.

adventures.name: The Dreaming Atelier
adventures.quest: Describe a feeling and the Atelier builds it — flawlessly, at scale, and frequently not the thing you meant.

adventures.name: Keep on the Token Borderlands
adventures.use_category: Security / red-team — the bestiary's Caves of Chaos
adventures.quest: A frontier keep at the edge of safe completion. Past its walls the Caves of Chaos teem with every jailbreak, injection, and hallucination — and the two who hold the line venture in first, on purpose.
